proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) is positively associated with pfs_months in this prostate cohort.
feature_057 (3-level ordinal, 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher levels correspond to shorter PFS.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; m=sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(df['feature_078'])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariable OLS: each one-unit increase in feature_078 is associated with +0.174 month longer pfs_months (Pearson r=0.856). Quintile means: 1.35, 2.75, 3.72, 4.70, 6.24 months — a near-monotonic gradient. By far the strongest single predictor.

Univariable OLS: each one-level increase in feature_057 is associated with -1.161 months PFS. Group means: f057=0 → 4.68 mo (n=17592), f057=1 → 3.49 mo (n=24971), f057=2 → 2.38 mo (n=7437). Clear monotonic prognostic gradient, behaves like a stage variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8 — pattern resembles a serum protein such as albumin) is positively associated with pfs_months.
feature_013 (continuous, highly right-skewed 0.12-3622, pattern resembles PSA) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable OLS: each one-unit increase in feature_009 is associated with +0.495 months longer PFS. Tertile means: 3.49, 3.78, 4.02. Direction matches expectation for a positive prognostic lab marker.

Univariable OLS slope = -0.00334 per unit (p≈0). Pearson r on raw scale = -0.153; r on log(feature_013+0.01) = -0.231 — relationship is stronger after log-transform, consistent with a heavy-tailed lab value (PSA-like).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (continuous, 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_067 (5-level ordinal taking values 6-10, pattern matches a Gleason score) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable OLS slope = -0.0749 per unit (p<1e-200). Pearson r = -0.143. Negative prognostic continuous variable.

Univariable OLS slope = -0.00622 per level, p=0.486. Group means by feature_067 (6,7,8,9,10): 3.71, 3.76, 3.74, 3.74, 3.70 — essentially flat. Despite the variable's appearance and clinical name (Gleason-like), it does NOT predict pfs_months in this cohort. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Self-reported race (feature_018: asian/black/hispanic/other/white) is associated with differences in mean pfs_months.
Insurance type (feature_045: medicare/medicaid/private/uninsured) is associated with differences in mean pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Race overall F-test F=0.564, p=0.689. Group means: white 3.741, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, asian 3.797, other 3.749. No meaningful between-race differences. Hypothesis refuted.
Insurance overall F-test F=0.344, p=0.794. Group means: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. No insurance-related disparity in this cohort. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_071 (5-level ordinal 0-4, distribution resembles ECOG performance status) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_026 (11-level ordinal 0-10, pattern resembles a count or score) is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Group means at f071 = 0,1,2,3,4: 3.743, 3.730, 3.772, 3.717, 3.762. No monotonic trend; differences within ±0.06 mo. Hypothesis refuted.
Group means span 3.69-3.84 across 11 levels with no monotonic trend (level 0 → 3.77, level 10 → 3.84). Variation is within noise. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (binary) is negatively associated with pfs_months — patients with feature_051=1 have shorter PFS than those with feature_051=0.
After adjustment for the strongest covariates (feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_009), feature_051 retains an independent negative effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: f051=0 (n=22519): mean PFS 4.031 mo; f051=1 (n=27481): mean PFS 3.509 mo. Two-sample t-test mean difference -0.521, p≈1e-181. Strong negative unadjusted association.

Adjusted OLS coefficient for feature_051 = -0.371 mo (p<1e-300). Effect persists after adjustment. Magnitude is large — equivalent to advancing roughly 2 years on the feature_078 gradient or one-third of a feature_057 stage level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 (binary, 10% prevalent) is associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
feature_039 (binary, 10% prevalent) is associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment for prognostic covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_109 = +0.151 mo (p=4e-68). Modest but highly significant positive effect on PFS after adjustment.
Adjusted coefficient for feature_039 = +0.137 mo (p=7e-57). Modest positive effect, similar in magnitude and direction to feature_109.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, the only ones with adjusted coefficients of clinically meaningful magnitude (|β|>0.10) on pfs_months are feature_051 (negative), feature_109 (positive), and feature_039 (positive); the remaining binaries are statistically uninformative noise.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(df[['feature_078','feature_057','feature_013','feature_009',c]])).fit() — record coef, p
analyses.result_summary: Sweep of adjusted models over all 77 binary features. Only three features had |coefficient|≥0.10 with p<1e-30: feature_051 (-0.371, p≈0), feature_109 (+0.151, p=4e-68), feature_039 (+0.137, p=7e-57). The next-strongest binaries (feature_027 +0.021, feature_043 -0.024, feature_079 -0.034) are all <0.05 month effects. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 act SYNERGISTICALLY on pfs_months: when both are 1, the effect is much larger than the sum of either alone.

analyses.code: df.groupby(['feature_109','feature_039'])['pfs_months'].mean(); model with feature_109, feature_039, and product term
analyses.result_summary: 2x2 cell means: (0,0) 3.742 (n=40435); (0,1) 3.668 (n=4569); (1,0) 3.671 (n=4467); (1,1) 5.216 (n=529). When either alone, no benefit (numerically slightly worse). When both present, +1.55 mo above expected additive value. Adjusted regression with interaction: feature_109 main = 0.003 (p=0.63), feature_039 main = -0.003 (p=0.66), feature_109×feature_039 = +1.440 (p≈0). The earlier 'main effects' were entirely driven by the synergistic combination. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is consistent across feature_057 strata (no qualitative interaction).
The effect of feature_078 on pfs_months attenuates at higher feature_057 levels (negative interaction): high f078 still helps but less so when f057 is high.

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of f051 by f057
analyses.result_summary: Within f057=0: f051 effect = -0.559 mo (p=1e-83). Within f057=1: -0.514 mo (p=5e-105). Within f057=2: -0.472 mo (p=5e-31). All same direction, similar magnitude. f051×f057 interaction in adjusted model coef = +0.026 (p=2.3e-5) — statistically detectable but tiny relative to the -0.38 main effect; clinically the negative effect of feature_051 holds across stages.

Adjusted interaction coefficient feature_078×feature_057 = -0.00942 (p=6e-208). Direction: each additional level of f057 reduces the per-unit f078 slope by ~0.009. Since unadjusted f078 slope is +0.174, this is ~5% relative attenuation per stage — statistically very strong but modest in absolute terms. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_009 on pfs_months is attenuated at higher feature_057 levels (negative interaction).

analyses.code: OLS with feature_009*feature_057 interaction term, adjusted
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction feature_009×feature_057 coefficient = -0.0223 (p=2.6e-4). Direction supports hypothesis: the protective per-unit effect of feature_009 decreases by 0.02 mo per stage level. Statistically significant but small in absolute magnitude vs. the +0.48 main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between feature_013 and feature_057 such that the negative slope of feature_013 on pfs_months differs by stage.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction coefficient = +0.00002 (p=0.56). No evidence of differential slope. Hypothesis refuted: feature_013's relationship with PFS is essentially uniform across f057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic feature_109×feature_039 effect is consistent across feature_057 strata (no three-way interaction with stage).

analyses.code: stratified analysis of feature_109 within each f057 stratum, and feature_109*f057 interaction (which captures combo effect because feature_039 alone is null)
analyses.result_summary: Stratified means f109 effect by stage: f057=0 +0.190 (p=8e-5), f057=1 +0.047 (p=0.23), f057=2 +0.159 (p=0.02). Direction positive in all strata, similar magnitude where significant. Two-way f109×f057 interaction coef = +0.002 (p=0.86) — not significant. The combo benefit is qualitatively present across stages. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 (the prognostically harmful binary) is consistent across self-reported races (no race × treatment interaction).

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of f051 by feature_018; nested F-test for race-by-treatment interaction
analyses.result_summary: Stratified differences (f051=1 minus f051=0) by race: white -0.525 (p=4e-120), black -0.506 (p=2e-22), hispanic -0.529 (p=6e-30), asian -0.535 (p=4e-13), other -0.366 (p=0.005). Nested F-test of 4 race×f051 interaction terms: F=0.41, p=0.80. Treatment effect is homogeneous across races. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 is consistent across insurance categories (no insurance × treatment interaction).

analyses.code: stratified t-tests of f051 by insurance
analyses.result_summary: Stratified differences (f051=1 minus f051=0) by insurance: medicare -0.522 (p=2e-78), private -0.506 (p=5e-69), medicaid -0.536 (p=1e-28), uninsured -0.601 (p=3e-11). All similar in magnitude and direction. No clinically meaningful insurance-related modification. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (Gleason-like 6-10) does not predict pfs_months even after adjustment for the dominant covariates — its lack of effect is real, not confounded.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for feature_067 in the full model = -0.0109 mo per level (p=4.7e-8). Although statistically significant in n=50000, the magnitude is trivial — moving from Gleason 6 to 10 changes PFS by only ~0.04 mo. Hypothesis broadly supported: in this cohort feature_067 is not a meaningful predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The five-level ordinal features feature_016, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064 (each with values 0-4) and feature_026 (0-10) are not associated with pfs_months in either direction.

analyses.code: df.groupby(col)['pfs_months'].mean() for col in [feature_016, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064, feature_026]
analyses.result_summary: All five ordinal features show flat group means within ±0.08 mo across their levels and no monotonic gradient (e.g., f016 0-4: 3.756, 3.744, 3.742, 3.745, 3.679; f064 0-4: 3.747, 3.733, 3.769, 3.716, 3.751; f026 0-10 spans 3.69-3.84 with non-monotonic pattern). These behave like noise variables. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious 9-feature model (feature_078, feature_057, feature_013, feature_009, feature_006, feature_051, feature_109, feature_039, feature_109×feature_039) explains a very large fraction of variance in pfs_months (R²>0.94).

analyses.result_summary: Final parsimonious OLS R² = 0.9514 (residual SD ≈ 0.45 mo on outcome SD 2.02). Coefficients: feature_078 +0.174, feature_057 -1.152, feature_013 -0.0027, feature_009 +0.479, feature_006 -0.076, feature_051 -0.376, feature_109 +0.003 (NS), feature_039 -0.003 (NS), feature_109×feature_039 +1.440. The combination interaction term carries virtually all of the f109/f039 prognostic information. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_018) is balanced across feature_051, feature_109, feature_039 — there is no systematic confounding by race in who receives or has these binary indicators.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_018'], df['feature_051'], normalize='index'); same for f109, f039
analyses.result_summary: Treatment/exposure rates by race look balanced (e.g., feature_051 prevalence ~55% in every race; feature_109 ~10% in every race). Together with the null race main effect on PFS and null race×f051 interaction, this rules out race as a confounder or effect modifier. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 retains an effect on pfs_months even within the most aggressive disease stratum (feature_057=2): the predictor is informative across the disease spectrum.

analyses.result_summary: Within-stratum correlations: f057=0 r=0.85; f057=1 r=0.85; f057=2 r=0.83 — feature_078 explains the bulk of within-stratum variance even in the worst-prognosis subgroup. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic feature_109+feature_039 combination provides PFS benefit on top of any feature_051 effect (additive across the two treatment-like signals).

analyses.code: OLS with feature_051, feature_109*feature_039, and (feature_051)*(f109*f039) triple interaction
analyses.result_summary: Adding a (feature_051)×(feature_109×feature_039) triple interaction term to the parsimonious model: triple-interaction coefficient ≈ -0.05, p>0.3 (not significant). The +1.44-month combination benefit and the -0.38 feature_051 penalty operate independently. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among lab-like continuous features, only feature_009, feature_013, and feature_006 carry meaningful prognostic information; the remaining numeric labs (feature_038, feature_014, feature_070, feature_044, feature_084, feature_028, feature_117, feature_056, feature_103, feature_094, feature_120, feature_019, feature_059, feature_003, feature_010, feature_101, feature_020, feature_090, feature_118, feature_054, feature_062, feature_031, feature_082, feature_075, feature_122, feature_005, feature_065, feature_055, feature_092) are uninformative.

analyses.code: for col in continuous lab features: r = df[col].corr(df['pfs_months']); rank by |r|
analyses.result_summary: After feature_078 (r=0.86), feature_013 (r=-0.15), feature_006 (r=-0.14), feature_009 (r=+0.12), all other continuous features have |r|<0.02 with pfs_months. Median |r| of remaining features ≈0.007 — indistinguishable from chance at this n. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months is approximately linear (no strong curvature); the simple linear term is sufficient.

analyses.result_summary: Quintile means: 1.35, 2.75, 3.72, 4.70, 6.24 mo — increments are 1.40, 0.97, 0.98, 1.54: largely linear with modest curvature at the extremes. Adding a quadratic term to the parsimonious model yields a coefficient of order 1e-4 with negligible R² gain (<0.001). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 is independent of the synergistic combination indicator (feature_109×feature_039); feature_051 remains harmful even when the combo is present.

analyses.code: stratified analysis: mean PFS by f051 within combo+ (f109=1 & f039=1) and within combo- subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Within combo+ (n=529): f051=0 mean PFS ≈ 5.49, f051=1 mean PFS ≈ 4.99; within combo- (n=49471): f051=0 mean ≈ 4.02, f051=1 mean ≈ 3.50. The f051 penalty (~-0.5 mo) is similar in both subgroups; the combo benefit (~+1.5 mo) layers on top. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final synthesis: pfs_months in this 50,000-patient prostate cohort is determined almost entirely by 8 features — feature_078, feature_057, feature_009, feature_013, feature_006, feature_051, and the synergistic pair (feature_109, feature_039) — while race, insurance, Gleason-like (feature_067), and the five-level ordinal features carry no clinically meaningful signal.

analyses.code: Compare R² of (a) full 8-feature parsimonious model with f109*f039 interaction vs (b) kitchen-sink model with all 122 candidate features
analyses.result_summary: Parsimonious 8-feature model with combo interaction: R² = 0.9514, residual SD ≈ 0.45 mo. Adding all 122 features: R² rises only marginally to ~0.952. Therefore essentially all explainable variance in pfs_months is captured by the eight identified features. Hypothesis supported.
